name: Return to the Tórtoles de Esgueva Reservoir
name_original: Vuelta al Embalse de Tórtoles de Esgueva
region_intro: Hiking around Tórtoles De Esgueva offers routes through a rural landscape characterized by the Esgueva river valley and its associated reservoirs. The terrain primarily consists of gentle slopes and open countryside, with some areas featuring agricultural land and scattered woodlands. This region provides opportunities for accessible outdoor activity, often following established paths and tracks. Best hiking trails in Tórtoles De Esgueva The most popular hiking route is Encinas Reservoir Loop, a 5.5 miles (8.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 20 minutes to complete. This moderate route circles the Encinas Reservoir, offering views of the water and surrounding fields. Another top favourite among local hikers is Embalse de Encinas de Esgueva, an easy 4.3 miles (6.9 km) path. This trail provides a shorter option around the reservoir, suitable for those seeking easy hikes Tórtoles De Esgueva. Local hikers also love the Part of the Mulberry and Valleys Trail - circular from Villovela de Esgueva, a 8.1 miles (13.0 km) trail leading through agricultural fields and small valleys, often completed in about 3 hours 34 minutes. Hiking around Tórtoles De Esgueva is defined by river valleys, reservoirs, and open rural landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to moderate long distance trails Tórtoles De Esgueva. The routes in Tórtoles De Esgueva are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 1 review. More than 10 hikers have used komoot to explore Tórtoles De Esgueva's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Tórtoles De Esgueva?
A: There are over 10 hiking routes in the Tórtoles De Esgueva area, offering a variety of options from easy walks to more challenging trails through the Esgueva river valley and surrounding countryside.

Q: Are there any easy hikes suitable for beginners or families in Tórtoles De Esgueva?
A: Yes, the region offers several easy routes. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077647364/embalse-de-encinas-de-esgueva" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Encinas de Esgueva&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 4.3-mile (6.9 km) path that provides a shorter, accessible walk around the reservoir.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Tórtoles De Esgueva?
A: The terrain primarily consists of gentle slopes and open countryside, often following established paths and tracks. You'll encounter agricultural land, scattered woodlands, and routes along the Esgueva river valley and its associated reservoirs.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1124627919/trail-between-vaso-and-espaldera-prc-bu-27-circular-from-anguix" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail between Vaso and Espaldera (PRC-BU 27) — circular from Anguix&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 5.9-mile (9.4 km) loop. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037354194/part-of-the-mulberry-and-valleys-trail-circular-from-villovela-de-esgueva" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Part of the Mulberry and Valleys Trail - circular from Villovela de Esgueva&lt;/a&gt;, which is 8.1 miles (13.0 km).

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions to see near the hiking trails?
A: While hiking, you can explore several points of interest. Consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4384200" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of the Zúñiga Family&lt;/a&gt; in Encinas de Esgueva, or the historic settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5506336" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tórtoles de Esgueva&lt;/a&gt; itself. There's also the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6156637" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monument to Spanish Pilots and Lance Armstrong&lt;/a&gt; in Antigüedad.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging hikes for experienced hikers?
A: The region offers several moderate routes, with the longest being the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037354194/part-of-the-mulberry-and-valleys-trail-circular-from-villovela-de-esgueva" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Part of the Mulberry and Valleys Trail - circular from Villovela de Esgueva&lt;/a&gt; at 8.1 miles (13.0 km). While there is one difficult route, most trails are moderate, providing good options for those seeking a longer day out.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Tórtoles De Esgueva?
A: The routes in Tórtoles De Esgueva are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Hikers often praise the peaceful rural landscapes and the well-maintained paths around the reservoirs.

Q: Is there a hike that specifically explores the Tórtoles de Esgueva Reservoir?
A: Yes, you can hike directly around the Tórtoles de Esgueva Reservoir. The route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1282068811/return-to-the-tortoles-de-esgueva-reservoir" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Return to the Tórtoles de Esgueva Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 5.4-mile (8.7 km) trail that offers views of the water and surrounding natural areas.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Tórtoles De Esgueva?
A: The region's climate makes spring and autumn ideal for hiking, with milder temperatures and pleasant scenery. Summer can be warm, so early mornings or late afternoons are recommended. Winter hiking is possible, but check local conditions as some paths might be muddy.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Tórtoles De Esgueva suitable for dog walking?
A: Generally, the rural and open nature of the trails in Tórtoles De Esgueva makes them suitable for dog walking. However, always keep dogs under control, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near livestock, and ensure you follow local regulations regarding pets.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Tórtoles De Esgueva?
A: Parking is typically available in or near the villages that serve as starting points for the trails, such as Tórtoles de Esgueva or Villovela de Esgueva. Specific parking information can often be found on individual route pages on komoot.
tips: Beautiful riverside town in the province of Burgos, full of monuments and places of cultural interest born around what was the Monastery of Santa María la Real since the dawn of the 12th century.

The Tórtoles de Esgueva reservoir is located in the place called "El Vallejo". This reservoir is surrounded by various vine and cereal crops, it has a long route through the Cerrato páramo and along the banks of the Esgueva river
